text: The panel CodeGate lead prompt (panelBody, scaffold-workflow.cjs ~349-355) leaves the lead's output contract undefined: the prompt orders 'FIX every Critical and High ... run the project build/lint (it MUST pass)' but never states what returning gatePassed=true requires, nor whether critical/high count REMAINING unresolved findings or FIXED ones — and the emitted schema requires only ['gatePassed'], leaving critical/high optional. The generated loop trusts gatePassed alone (:357), the throw message (:359) assumes 'unresolved' semantics the lead was never given, the deterministic skip/no-findings paths (:310, :342) hard-code critical:0/high:0 with none-remaining semantics the lead is not held to, and results.codeGate (:360) is persisted by checkpoint() into the durable state JSON audit trail (workflow-report.cjs renders only panel.raw/deduped/confirmed, so the garbage counts land in the state file, not the report body). Fix in the GENERATOR (regenerate; test-scaffold.cjs stays green as the floor): add a contract sentence to the lead prompt containing the verbatim needles 'gatePassed=true ONLY' (if every Critical/High is fixed or skipped-with-explicit-reason AND build/lint passes) and 'left UNRESOLVED' (critical/high = counts left UNRESOLVED after your fixes), and add 'critical' and 'high' to the lead schema's required array. Natural same-commit stretch (not part of the frozen check): apply the identical sentence to the single-agent gateLoop CodeGate (~:467-473) and merged Review (~:528-538), and consider a structured resolved list so the round>1 CARRY_DECL (:270) stops depending on last.summary prose for convergence. Discriminating check (RED on current generator, GREEN after): generate a panel loop via node scripts/scaffold-workflow.cjs --name wcheck --phases Implement --profile delivery --no-dod --out <scratch>/wcheck.js --force (delivery hard-errors without --dod-file or --no-dod), slice the generated source between the index of "inWorktree('codegate-lead')" and the 'codegate:lead-r' label plus its schema line, and assert INSIDE that slice (a) both exact needles 'gatePassed=true ONLY' and 'left UNRESOLVED' appear (paraphrase-matching is not deterministic) and (b) the lead schema's required array contains 'critical' and 'high' alongside 'gatePassed' — scoped to the lead schema specifically, not the guard/finding/score schemas; also assert the deterministic paths still emit 'critical: 0, high: 0' in both the guard-skip and zero-confirmed results so a lazy fix cannot resolve the ambiguity by deleting the counts. RED today because the generated lead prompt carries no contract sentence and the generated lead schema's required list is exactly ['gatePassed'].

The panel CodeGate lead prompt (scaffold-workflow.cjs :351) tells the lead it 'may skip a finding ONLY with an explicit reason it is wrong', but the lead's output schema (:355) is exactly {gatePassed, critical, high, summary} and the SCHEMA helper emits additionalProperties:false (:227), so skip reasons have no structured home — they get crammed into the one-line summary or silently dropped — and since results.codeGate = { ...last, panel } (:360) is all the checkpoint/report ever see (workflow-report.cjs :124-125 renders only the raw/deduped/confirmed counts), a human reviewing the PR cannot audit which confirmed (confidence>=80) findings the lead overruled or why. Fix: add a 'skippedFindings' array (items requiring title and reason) to the lead schema — NOT named 'skipped', which already exists as a BOOLEAN on the guard-skip path's results.codeGate (:310), so reusing that key would give it two types and a naive whole-file grep for 'skipped' already passes today, breaking the RED claim — instruct the lead in the prompt to record every skipped finding there, let it ride the existing { ...last } spread into results.codeGate, and render it in workflow-report.cjs beside the panel counts so the audit trail reaches the report/PR body. Discriminating check (RED on current generator, GREEN after): generate a delivery workflow (panel default ON; use --no-dod), slice the generated source at the codegate-lead agent call ('codegate:lead-r' label back to "inWorktree('codegate-lead')"), and assert within that slice that the lead schema declares a skippedFindings array whose item schema requires title and reason and that the lead prompt instructs recording each skipped finding in that array; PLUS a report-path assertion — run workflow-report.cjs against a fixture state whose results.codeGate.skippedFindings contains one entry and assert the rendered markdown includes that entry's title and reason (without this a schema/prompt-only fix turns the check green while the audit trail still never reaches the report). RED today because the string skippedFindings appears nowhere in the generated lead call nor in workflow-report.cjs.

The panel CodeGate's confidence scorers filter on confidence only (scaffold-workflow.cjs :339, confidence >= 80) with no severity criterion, so verified-real Medium/Low findings reach the lead — but the lead prompt (:351) only instructs 'FIX every Critical and High' and permits skipping a finding 'ONLY with an explicit reason it is wrong', so a confirmed Medium at confidence 90 has no legal disposition: it is neither fixed, nor skipped-with-reason (it is not wrong), nor carried into results.codeGate (the lead schema :355 holds only gatePassed/critical/high/summary; :360 adds only panel counts), and workflow-report.cjs (:124-125) prints only the raw/deduped/confirmed count line, so it silently vanishes from the run's audit trail. Fix in the generator: add a lead-prompt line giving Medium/Low a disposition (fix only when the fix is trivial and zero-risk; otherwise return them untouched as known-open with file:line) plus a knownOpen array in the lead schema (items with at least file, line, severity, title) that rides the existing { ...last } spread into results.codeGate, and render a known-open line in workflow-report.cjs next to the panel counts. Discriminating check (RED on current generator, GREEN after), three scoped assertions on a generated panel loop (--phases Implement --profile delivery --no-dod) plus one on workflow-report.cjs: (a) extract the lead-prompt block — the substring between 'review-panel LEAD' and 'FINDINGS (JSON)' — and assert it matches /[Mm]edium/ AND /known[- ]?open/i (do NOT grep the whole generated file: the dimension prompt at :318 already contains 'Medium', so an unscoped regex passes today); (b) assert the codegate-lead SCHEMA call declares a knownOpen array property whose item schema includes file, line, severity, title, and that the results.codeGate assignment still spreads the lead result (matches /results\.codeGate = \{ \.\.\.last/), which is what carries knownOpen through; (c) assert workflow-report.cjs references knownOpen and, given a fixture state whose results.codeGate.knownOpen is non-empty, emits a known-open line in the rendered report — without (c) a prompt+schema-only fix goes green while the finding stays invisible in the run report, which is half the concern. RED today because the lead-prompt block mentions only Critical and High and knownOpen appears nowhere in the generated script or workflow-report.cjs.

Every CodeGate variant both fixes findings and self-declares the gate passed on the honor system: the panel lead prompt says 'run the project build/lint (it MUST pass)' (scaffold-workflow.cjs :351) and the single-agent CodeGate / merged Review say 'Run the project build/lint — it MUST pass' (:471, :533), yet every variant's schema is just {gatePassed, critical, high, summary} with only gatePassed required (:355, :472, :537) and the loops accept passed = last && last.gatePassed verbatim — nothing requires evidence the build actually ran, so a lead can go green without ever invoking it. Fix (cheap honesty anchor, not a second verifier agent): add required buildCmd (exact command run, string) and buildExit (number) fields to the CodeGate/Review gate schemas, have the prompts demand them — wording must handle projects with no build/lint to avoid forcing fabrication: 'report the exact command run as buildCmd and its numeric exit code as buildExit; if the project has no build/lint, run the closest verification command (e.g. the test suite) and report that instead' — and make the emitted pass conditions reject gatePassed:true when buildExit !== 0. Scope: gateLoop (:276) is shared ONLY by CodeGate and Review (TestGate has its own inline loop :506-521 and is out of scope), so apply the condition inside gateLoop plus the panel lead's inline passed = ... at :357; the panel's zero-confirmed early pass (:342) synthesizes gatePassed:true without any lead agent or build and must stay untouched — the check must not assert build evidence on that synthesized object. Discriminating check (RED on current generator, GREEN after): generate three loops — delivery-profile with --no-dod (panel lead), --phases Work --enforce-code (single-agent CodeGate via gateLoop), and --phases Work --merge-gates --enforce-code --enforce-tests (merged Review) — and assert in each emitted gate block (scoped to the codegate-lead / codegate / review agent calls, not TestGate) that the schema string declares required buildCmd and buildExit properties, the gate prompt instructs reporting the exact build/lint command and its exit status, and the pass condition checks buildExit === 0 alongside gatePassed. RED today because the emitted scripts contain no buildCmd/buildExit at all — the gate schemas carry only gatePassed/critical/high/summary and the loops test gatePassed alone.

test-scaffold.cjs defends the 799-line generator only with sampled structural asserts — node --check (:108), emitted-vs-reported phase order (:111-119), the conductor-purity string scan (:133-141), and ~35 targeted regexes (:165-262) over an 18-combo matrix (:46-67) — so a refactor can silently alter any emitted worker prompt or conductor logic the regexes do not happen to touch and stay green, in a generator with ~30 flags and real interaction rules (--merge-gates ignored under --cycle :69-70; --review-panel WARNS without a CodeGate :115-116; --model-think/--model-work rejected outside --model-mode auto :163-171) whose config space the matrix only samples. Output is byte-deterministic for fixed args (verified: two runs cmp equal; no Date.now/Math.random executes at generation time; determinism holds across differing cwd, --out paths, and input-file locations, so no normalization/scrubbing is needed), which makes golden-file snapshots cheap and honest. Fix: commit fixtures under scripts/snapshots/<label>.js for a small canonical combo set (default bare, --profile lite, --profile delivery, --cycle, --enforce-code --review-panel, --model-mode auto) TOGETHER WITH pinned input fixtures (e.g. scripts/snapshots/inputs/prompts.json and dod.json — the lite/delivery combos require --dod-file and DoD criteria + prompts are baked into output verbatim, so byte-equality is ill-defined without pinned inputs; input file PATHS are not embedded in output, so tmp-dir generation against the committed input content is safe); make test-scaffold.cjs regenerate each combo and byte-compare against its fixture, with an explicit update/regen path (e.g. --update-snapshots) so intentional changes ride the same commit as the generator change. Discriminating check (RED on current generator, GREEN after): (a) primary — the check itself regenerates each canonical combo from the committed input fixtures with fixed args and asserts the output byte-equals the committed scripts/snapshots/<label>.js fixture; (b) negative control proving the harness comparison is live rather than decorative — copy the committed fixtures to a temp dir, flip one byte in one fixture, run test-scaffold.cjs's snapshot comparison against the tampered copy via a snapshot-dir override the fix must support (SNAPSHOT_DIR env or --snapshot-dir) and assert non-zero exit. RED today because no snapshot fixture exists anywhere under the skill (grep for snapshot/golden across scripts/ and evals/ finds none) and test-scaffold.cjs performs no byte-equality comparison of generated output.

Every worker prompt in scaffold-workflow.cjs lives three-to-four quoting layers deep — generator template literal -> escaped emitted runtime template literal -> English prose -> shell inside the prose (triple-backslashed backticks at :713-714 and :515), with escTpl (:219) existing solely to push data down one layer safely and DOD_DECL/CARRY_DECL (:269-270) hand-writing emitted template-literal source inside plain JS strings; editing any prompt requires knowing which interpolation layer you are in, and a wrong-layer interpolation or newline yields a subtly-wrong generated script rather than a parse error (node --check in test-scaffold.cjs only catches syntax breaks). Best sequenced AFTER scaffold-golden-snapshots so byte-equality fixtures can prove the extraction is behavior-preserving. Fix: extract the static prose of the worker prompts into plain template files under scripts/prompts/ and have the generator load them at generation time with mechanical substitution. Templates MAY carry emitted-runtime dollar-brace expressions verbatim (the loader applies only the generator-escaping layer) plus a distinct generation-time placeholder syntax (e.g. {{NAME}}) for generator-computed fragments — a pure named-placeholder scheme is NOT required, since many interpolations are runtime JS expressions inside the emitted literal. Byte-identical emitted output across the whole test-scaffold.cjs matrix is a fix-time requirement verified by the run's reviewer (diff freshly generated matrix outputs before/after), not part of the frozen check — the matrix staying green is the enforceable floor. Discriminating check (RED on current generator, GREEN after), using one distinctive prose sentinel per extracted prompt family, all currently inline in scaffold-workflow.cjs: 'DoD BASELINE (pre-work measurement)' (:406), 'RED (test-first):' (:430), 'VERIFY (GREEN):' (:444), 'PATH-GAP: the ACs do NOT cover' (:455), 'Review AND fix the changes on branch' (:471/:532), 'RE-VERIFY: after IMPROVE/SIMPLIFY' (:482), 'Assess the changes on branch' (:494), 'Write an implementation summary' (:546), 'DoD VERIFY (round' (:561), 'DoD FIX (round' (:576), 'Produce reviewer-facing delivery artifacts' (:596), 'Push the branch and open a PR' (:612), 'Update tracker ticket' (:624), 'READ-ONLY panel review' (:316), 'Score 0-100 how confident' (:130), 'Work ONLY inside the git worktree' (:713). Assert (a) each sentinel appears in some scripts/prompts/ template file, (b) NONE appears inline in scaffold-workflow.cjs, (c) scripts/prompts/ contains at least 12 files (so a minimal 3-prompt extraction cannot go green), and (d) a generated delivery script — generate exactly like the matrix's delivery entry: --profile delivery --dod-file <fixture> --prompts-file <fixture> --phases Implement (delivery hard-errors without --dod-file) — still contains every sentinel belonging to a delivery-emitted phase (substitution wired correctly; check TestGate's 'Assess the changes on branch' in a separate --phases Work --enforce-tests generation, since delivery's cycle replaces TestGate), with the existing test-scaffold.cjs matrix green. RED today because all sentinels live only inline in scaffold-workflow.cjs and no scripts/prompts/ dir exists.
